name: syndigo-design-system
description: 55 production-ready React components with Figma parity

required: Use Text component for all typography
forbidden: No HTML elements directly for UI (<button>, <input>, <select>)
No inline font styles (fontFamily, fontSize, fontWeight)
No custom wrapper components without approval
No Tailwind classes for Syndigo components

beforeCoding.step1_listComponents: FIRST review available Syndigo components by category: Form & Input (11), Layout & Containers (10), Navigation (10), Data Display (13), Feedback & States (5), Actions & Controls (3), Advanced (3). Component names MUST match exact names from these lists.
beforeCoding.step2_estimateComplexity: Estimate component count: 'For [screen type], I estimate [X] components: [Y] form/input, [Z] layout, [W] data display, [V] feedback/states'. This catches missing components early.
beforeCoding.step3_determineFormat: Choose format based on complexity: SIMPLE (1-5 components) = single table, MEDIUM (6-15) = single table with section comments, COMPLEX (16+) = separate tables per section (Header, Filters, Content, Modals, States).
beforeCoding.step4_createMapping: Create Component Mapping Table in EXACT markdown format with 5 columns: UI Element | Syndigo Component | Required Props | Optional Props | Notes. Required Props MUST include state bindings (value/onChange for inputs), event handlers (onClick/onSubmit), and conditional props (disabled/loading/error). Optional Props include styling, labels, placeholders. Notes explain non-obvious decisions.
beforeCoding.step5_validate: Self-validate using 12-point checklist: COMPLETENESS (all elements, edge cases, interactions, data display), ACCURACY (exact names, state bindings, event handlers, conditional props), QUALITY (realistic values, obvious types, clear state management, explanatory notes). Show validation score: X/12 checks passed.
beforeCoding.step6_present: Present mapping with: title 'Component Mapping Table', validation score, estimated component count, all mapping tables, then EXPLICIT statement 'WAITING FOR YOUR APPROVAL - Please respond with: APPROVED, Change [X] to [Y], Start over, or Add [element]. I will NOT start coding until you explicitly approve.'
beforeCoding.step7_handleFeedback: If 'APPROVED' → proceed to implementation. If change requested → update specific rows, re-validate, show revised mapping with new score, wait again. If 'Start over' → ask clarifying questions, return to step 1. If 'STOP' → apologize, return to step 1.
beforeCoding.criticalRule: NEVER write code before receiving explicit 'APPROVED' or 'Looks good' response. If you skip this workflow and user says 'STOP', immediately apologize and return to step 1.

implementation.strictAdherence: Use ONLY components from approved mapping table - no additions, no substitutions, no custom components
implementation.propsMatch: Props MUST match mapping specifications exactly - Required Props from mapping become actual props in code
implementation.edgeCases: Implement ALL edge cases from mapping (loading, error, empty states) - if not in mapping, ask before adding
implementation.codeStructure: Add comment at top: '// Component Mapping Reference: See approved table above // Estimated components: [N]'. Group code by mapping sections. Add inline comments referencing mapping rows.
requiredRules: Use <Text> component for all text (never inline fontFamily/fontSize/fontWeight)
Use design tokens for colors: var(--color-blue-500), var(--color-text-primary), etc.
forbiddenActions: Never use hardcoded colors (#2d75e2, rgb(), rgba())
Never create custom wrapper components without explicit approval
Never add components not in approved mapping
Never use Tailwind classes for Syndigo components

validation.preImplementation: Before coding: mapping approved? All components from list? Props complete? Edge cases included?
postImplementation: All components from approved mapping used?
No components NOT in mapping added?
All edge cases from mapping implemented?
validation.ifValidationFails: Fix issues immediately before presenting code to user. Do not show broken code.

errorRecovery.ifSkippedMapping: User says 'STOP. Show me the Component Mapping Table first' → Apologize, return to step 1 (list components), complete full workflow, do NOT use any code already written
errorRecovery.ifWrongComponent: User says 'That component doesn't exist' → Check available components list, show correct alternatives from list, update mapping, wait for approval
errorRecovery.ifIncompleteProps: User says 'Missing [prop]' → Update mapping table to add prop, show revised mapping, wait for new approval
errorRecovery.ifNoApproval: If you proceeded without approval → Stop immediately, show mapping retroactively, apologize, ask for approval to continue or restart

avoidThese: Creating mapping from memory (ALWAYS review component list first)
Vague props like 'label, value, onChange' (SHOW actual values: value={email}, onChange={setEmail})
Missing edge cases (ALWAYS include loading, error, empty state components)
Guessing component names (USE exact names from list)
Single table for 20+ components (BREAK into sections)
Ignoring user changes (UPDATE mapping and wait for new approval)
